reference_models: Mayo Clinic; Cleveland Clinic; NHS; MedlinePlus; MSD Manuals
search_discovery: Internal search, A–Z/topic navigation, task-based pages; exact implementations to audit
comparison: Usually educational rather than multi-provider comparison
provider_inventory: Owned-system providers for hospital models; none for government references
availability_booking: Owned-system care entry or public-service navigation; varies
reviews_ugc: Usually limited or absent on editorial content
price_transparency: Usually limited; not the core value proposition
AI: Current public AI features DATA REQUIRED
3D_AR: Media/illustration may exist; current interactive 3D DATA REQUIRED
trust_governance: Institutional authorship, medical review, corrections, cited sources and update controls are the benchmark questions
monetization: Care delivery, public funding, advertising or institutional support depending on model
hea_lth_match: Entity depth, plain-language summaries, authors/reviewers, evidence and update dates
hea_lth_exceed: Hebrew/Israeli terminology, payer routes, transparent decision tools and neutral links to verified supply
hea_lth_avoid: Publishing breadth faster than reviewer/evidence capacity; copying institutional prose
build_buy_partner: Build ontology/editorial workflow; license terminology only after rights review; partner for specialist review
evidence_status: Archetype hypothesis; page/template audit pending Wave 2 and ontology audit Wave 4

reference_models: Zocdoc; Doctolib; Docplanner; Top Doctors; Practo
knowledge_taxonomy: Specialty, reason-for-visit, procedure, insurance/payer, location, language and availability entities
search_discovery: Facets plus high-intent specialty/location/service landing pages
comparison: Provider profiles, availability, insurance/payer, languages, experience signals and reviews
provider_inventory: Core asset; verification, freshness, ownership, capacity and geographic density determine value
availability_booking: Real-time or request-based scheduling, confirmation, reminders and change/cancellation states
reviews_ugc: Verified-encounter eligibility, moderation, fraud, dispute and provider-response controls required
price_transparency: Often incomplete; exact service/price and inclusions need structured observations
commerce_quote: Booking/lead transaction rather than product checkout
AI: Potential navigation/intake/admin support; exact competitor scope DATA REQUIRED
trust_governance: License verification, sponsor separation, ranking methodology, review policy, privacy and service-status controls
monetization: Provider subscriptions, SaaS, booking/lead fees, labeled sponsorship
hea_lth_match: Structured provider data, verification, response/availability, booking states and professional dashboard
hea_lth_exceed: Israeli registry re-checks, payer-route accuracy, transparent ranking, quality guardrails without unsupported clinical scores
hea_lth_avoid: Empty city/specialty doorway pages, pay-to-rank opacity, collecting medical history in first contact
build_buy_partner: Buy/partner for calendar connectivity and identity verification where credible; build data governance/routing differentiation
evidence_status: Zocdoc discovery baseline only; official audits and Israeli supply interviews pending

knowledge_taxonomy: Concern, anatomy, procedure, technique, product/device, provider, location, recovery, risk and price entities
comparison: Treatments, clinicians, clinics, reviews, price, recovery, suitability and alternatives
provider_inventory: Specialist/clinic credentials, privileges, devices, techniques and media rights
reviews_ugc: High-value but high-risk; verified encounters, incentives, editing and dispute controls
price_transparency: High commercial value but methodologically difficult; package scope and variability must be explicit
accounts: Saved research, consultation inquiries, photos/consents only under secure governed workflows
commerce_quote: Consultation/lead; selected aftercare products may transact separately
AI: Education/search may be lower risk; outcome prediction is high risk
3D_AR: Potentially valuable patient-specific simulation with strict consent and no-outcome-guarantee controls
trust_governance: Specialist review, before/after consent, image integrity, advertising disclosure, review authenticity and conflict management
monetization: Provider subscriptions/leads, labeled sponsorship, concierge, selected products
hea_lth_match: Decision depth, visuals, provider discovery, transparent price method and consultation journey
hea_lth_exceed: Evidence-linked risk/alternatives, Israeli credential checks, audit trails, unedited-media policy and independent ranking
hea_lth_avoid: Outcome promises, manipulated media, undisclosed incentives, generic price claims or unreviewed suitability advice
build_buy_partner: Build editorial/provider layer; license simulation from governed vendor; partner for clinical media and consent tooling
evidence_status: Archetype hypothesis; official competitor and Israeli legal audits pending

reference_models: Prenuvo; Ezra; Function Health; Neko Health; Human Longevity
knowledge_taxonomy: Test, biomarker, modality, evidence, indication, limitation, interval, incidental finding, provider and follow-up
search_discovery: Package-led education, condition/risk themes and premium brand acquisition
comparison: Tests/packages, evidence, exclusions, locations, turnaround, follow-up and price
provider_inventory: Labs, imaging centers, clinicians, care coordinators and referral pathways
availability_booking: Package purchase/booking plus preparation, result and follow-up states
reviews_ugc: Secondary; requires care because outcomes and clinical claims are sensitive
price_transparency: Usually package pricing; must specify inclusions, downstream costs and limitations
accounts: Longitudinal results, reports, consents and sensitive health data create medical-record-like obligations
commerce_quote: Direct package purchase/subscription; may require clinical eligibility/referral
AI: May summarize or analyze data; validation, oversight and device/regulatory status are critical
3D_AR: Imaging/data visualization may add value; patient-specific analysis is high risk
trust_governance: Clinical evidence, appropriateness, false positives, incidental findings, escalation, privacy, clinician oversight and claims
hea_lth_match: Transparent evidence/limitations and neutral service comparison before selling coordination
hea_lth_exceed: Israeli payer/referral reality, independent evidence map, downstream pathway and explicit data minimization
hea_lth_avoid: Selling broad screening as universally beneficial, overtesting, opaque AI interpretation or unmanaged results repository
build_buy_partner: Partner with licensed labs/imaging/clinicians; do not build clinical hardware or interpretation engine early
evidence_status: High-risk hypothesis; clinical systematic review and Israeli regulation/supply research pending

reference_models: Ada; Infermedica; K Health; Doctolib AI
knowledge_taxonomy: Symptoms, intents, urgency, services, provider capabilities and evidence provenance
comparison: Can explain options; must not silently rank clinical choices without validated criteria
provider_inventory: Navigation quality depends on fresh structured service/availability data
availability_booking: Administrative scheduling can be lower risk; clinical routing/triage is higher risk
price_transparency: Can retrieve approved price data; must show provenance/date and uncertainty
accounts: Personalization increases sensitivity and consent/security burden
commerce_quote: May assist but should not autonomously commit high-risk transactions or share health data
AI: Core; split lower-risk retrieval/navigation, controlled administration and regulated clinical use
trust_governance: Grounding, provenance, human oversight, prompt-injection controls, evaluation, incident reporting and regulatory classification
monetization: Enterprise licensing, care delivery, administrative efficiency or conversion assistance
hea_lth_match: Citation-grounded search, Hebrew synonyms, neutral navigation, content/provider QA
hea_lth_exceed: Per-answer provenance, risk-class gating, red-team results, data minimization, model/vendor portability and human escalation
hea_lth_avoid: General-purpose LLM diagnosis, triage, treatment or medication advice; autonomous clinical claims
build_buy_partner: Build retrieval/orchestration and evaluations; buy models as interchangeable components; partner for any regulated clinical engine
evidence_status: Governance baseline supported by WHO/NIST/OWASP; vendor and regulatory audits pending

reference_models: Crisalix; Canfield VECTRA; model-viewer; glTF; WebXR
knowledge_taxonomy: Asset, anatomy, procedure, device, version, source, consent, disclaimer and compatibility metadata
search_discovery: Embedded on education, consultation or equipment pages
comparison: Side-by-side anatomy/procedure/device views; patient-specific simulation is distinct
availability_booking: Often supports consultation conversion rather than booking itself
accounts: Patient images/models require strong consent, retention, access and deletion controls
commerce_quote: 3D device inspection may support B2B quote; AR may support eligible product conversion
AI: Optional segmentation/generation; increases validation and privacy risk
trust_governance: Clinical accuracy, asset provenance, consent, disclaimer, accessibility fallback, performance, device support and liability
monetization: Licensed software, provider conversion, product/device sales support
hea_lth_match: Educational anatomy/procedure and device visualization with fallbacks
hea_lth_exceed: Versioned medically reviewed asset registry, accessible narrative equivalent and measured decision value
hea_lth_avoid: Generic AI art, implied outcome guarantees, unconsented face/body processing and heavy assets that damage performance
build_buy_partner: Build delivery pipeline; license accurate assets/simulation; partner for patient-specific clinical tools
evidence_status: Technical and vendor research pending Wave 7

knowledge_taxonomy: Category, brand, model, specifications, compatibility, intended use, regulatory documents, condition, service, warranty and supplier
search_discovery: Faceted catalog, account pricing, part/model lookup, wanted listings and procurement content
comparison: Specifications, condition, warranty, total cost, service, availability and supplier
availability_booking: Quote/RFP, installation/training/service scheduling rather than clinical appointments
reviews_ugc: Buyer/seller feedback may help but needs fraud/dispute controls
price_transparency: Mixed: checkout for standard items; quote for complex/high-ticket equipment
accounts: Verified professional, organization, procurement roles, teams, budgets and reorder history
commerce_quote: Checkout, quote/RFP, leasing, service, parts, trade-in and potentially auctions
AI: Catalog normalization, compatibility assistance and quote triage can be lower risk if grounded
trust_governance: Professional eligibility, supplier identity, regulatory status, condition evidence, title, warranty, fraud, tax, installation and service
monetization: Subscription, margin, listing, quote/transaction, financing and service-contract revenue
hea_lth_match: Structured catalog, verified suppliers, quote workflow, service/warranty and professional permissions
hea_lth_exceed: Israel-specific regulatory evidence, lifecycle/service data, total-cost comparison and assisted high-ticket transactions
hea_lth_avoid: Listing devices without status/docs, unsupported compatibility, unverified used condition or consumer checkout for professional-only products
build_buy_partner: Build marketplace data/workflows; partner with distributors, finance, logistics and service firms; buy PIM/search components as needed
evidence_status: Official competitor and Israeli supplier/regulatory research pending

reference_models: Curated retailer/marketplace models; Israeli analogues DATA REQUIRED
knowledge_taxonomy: Device/product, intended use, claims, user, contraindications, classification, warranty, installation, consumables and support
search_discovery: Category, problem, brand/model, comparison, reviews and price
comparison: Specifications, intended use, evidence, usability, service and total cost
availability_booking: Delivery, installation, training or service scheduling where needed
price_transparency: Checkout categories can show current price; high-risk/complex products may require advice or quote
accounts: Orders, warranties, subscriptions, returns, support and consent where applicable
commerce_quote: Owned inventory, marketplace, dropship, affiliate or curated partner depending category
AI: Grounded comparison explanation only; no patient-specific product prescribing
trust_governance: Classification, approved claims, authenticity, recall/status monitoring, returns, warranty, support and recommendation boundaries
monetization: Margin, marketplace fee, affiliate/referral, subscriptions and service
hea_lth_match: Curated eligible categories, evidence-led comparison and strong aftercare
hea_lth_exceed: Product-status ledger, claim moderation, verified support network and knowledge-to-product transparency
hea_lth_avoid: Prescription/restricted items, medical recommendations from generic AI, drop-shipping without service or unverified claims
build_buy_partner: Start curated partner/affiliate or marketplace; own inventory only after demand and operations validate
evidence_status: Category-by-category Israeli legal, margin and fulfillment research pending Wave 6

reference_models: GoodRx; official drug indexes; licensed pharmacies
knowledge_taxonomy: Drug, active ingredient, strength, form, manufacturer, prescription status, indication, warning, interaction, price/status and source
search_discovery: Drug/ingredient/brand, price, leaflet, shortage and availability
comparison: Official price/status and licensed alternatives only within legal/clinical boundaries
provider_inventory: Licensed pharmacies and professional guidance pathways
availability_booking: Links/referrals to licensed operators; prescription fulfillment model must be exact
reviews_ugc: Generally unsuitable for clinical efficacy claims; service reviews require controls
price_transparency: High utility if official/current and methodologically clear
accounts: Reminders or saved drugs increase privacy sensitivity; avoid creating medication histories casually
commerce_quote: No prescription-drug commerce without exact licensed model; OTC/device categories separately classified
AI: Reference retrieval and plain-language explanation may be possible; medication advice is high risk
trust_governance: Official index, current leaflets, prescription/OTC boundary, pharmacy licensing, substitution, shortages, pharmacovigilance and advertising
monetization: Licensed partnership/referral or none; conflicts require disclosure
hea_lth_exceed: Hebrew synonym/ingredient graph, source freshness and safety routing
hea_lth_avoid: Automated substitutions, dose advice, prescription sales or availability claims without licensed/current data
build_buy_partner: Use official sources and licensed pharmacy partners; do not build clinical recommendation engine
evidence_status: Israeli official-source and legal model pending Wave 1/6

reference_models: Maccabi; Clalit and other HMO/SHABAN/private-insurance surfaces
knowledge_taxonomy: Plan, benefit, service, provider, eligibility, waiting period, referral, co-pay, reimbursement and document
comparison: Cross-plan comparison is valuable but highly time-sensitive and condition-dependent
provider_inventory: Network status and payer route are central
availability_booking: Member booking may be integrated; external marketplace requires separate verification
price_transparency: Co-pays/reimbursement rules may be available but vary
accounts: Authenticated member accounts and sensitive records
commerce_quote: Insurance/finance sales subject to regulatory and conflict review
AI: Administrative navigation may help only with current sourced rules
trust_governance: Official plan documents, effective dates, eligibility, disclaimers, secure authentication and no implied coverage
monetization: Membership/insurance/care delivery; Hea-lth monetization must not distort eligibility information
hea_lth_match: Neutral payer-route explanation and source/date display
hea_lth_exceed: Cross-payer task journeys, document checklist and concierge without collecting excess health data
hea_lth_avoid: Guaranteeing reimbursement, outdated benefits, lead routing disguised as neutral coverage advice
build_buy_partner: Build explanatory layer; partner/integrate only where official data/API/contract exists
